name: Allora Network
one_liner: Decentralized AI inference network building self-improving ML models through economic incentives, competing with Bittensor subnets.
thesis:
Allora Network is building a decentralized AI inference network where models improve through economic competition. Conceptually similar to Bittensor but with a different architectural approach. The network uses a topic-based system where workers submit inferences and reputers evaluate quality, with economic rewards flowing to the most accurate participants. This creates a self-improving system where model quality increases as more capital is staked.

Allora appears in the broader AI x Crypto space referenced across the research documents but receives less specific coverage than the major players (Bittensor, Gensyn, Akash, Render). The project has raised venture funding but specific amounts are not detailed in the source documents. The competitive positioning against Bittensor's 128 subnets and established system is the primary challenge. Allora must demonstrate superior inference quality or lower costs to attract users from the incumbent.

The absence of a live token and limited coverage in the research documents mean this entry carries higher uncertainty than the scored alternatives. The project is included because it represents an additional data point in the decentralized AI inference category, which the research identifies as the strongest pattern match to DeFi Summer 2020. If the category thesis plays out, multiple winners may emerge from different architectural approaches.

The risk profile is dominated by execution uncertainty and competitive pressure. Bittensor has first-mover advantage with 128 active subnets, Grayscale ETF filings, and institutional attention. For Allora to capture meaningful value, it needs to ship a differentiated product that demonstrates clear advantages over the established competition. A high bar in a sector already experiencing severe valuation compression.

name: DAWN
one_liner: Trustless last-mile wireless network with Proof-of-Bandwidth consensus delivering gigabit speeds at 70% lower cost than traditional telecom.
thesis:
DAWN is building a trustless last-mile wireless infrastructure network using a novel Proof-of-Bandwidth consensus mechanism. The value proposition is stark. Gigabit-speed internet at 70% lower cost than traditional telecom providers. Last-mile connectivity is the most expensive and least competitive segment of the telecom stack, making it a compelling target for DePIN disruption.

The Proof-of-Bandwidth consensus is technically new. Rather than proving compute work or storage capacity, DAWN nodes prove actual bandwidth delivery. This creates a verifiable quality-of-service guarantee that differentiates it from Helium's IoT/5G approach and other wireless DePINs that focus on coverage rather than throughput.

The DePIN sector context is favorable. 423 active projects with $19.2B combined market cap and 41.8M devices demonstrate growing infrastructure. The World Economic Forum's $3.5T projection by 2028 specifically highlights connectivity as a key growth area. DAWN's gigabit-speed target positions it for the bandwidth-intensive applications driving demand. Video streaming, cloud gaming, remote work, and AI model deployment at the edge.

As a pre-token project, DAWN is not yet investable through public markets. The wireless DePIN subcategory has proven viable through Helium's network of nearly 1M hotspots, but last-mile broadband is significantly more complex than IoT connectivity. Regulatory requirements for wireless spectrum, local permitting, and interference management create barriers that pure software DePINs do not face.

Successful gigabit-speed demonstrations in pilot markets proving technical viability
Telecom cost pressure driving consumer demand for 70%-cheaper alternatives

name: Perovskite Solar (Oxford PV / First Solar proxy)
one_liner: Perovskite-silicon tandem solar hit commercialization. Oxford PV shipped first modules, Hanwha hit 28.6% efficiency.
thesis:
Perovskite solar technology hit its commercialization inflection in 2024-2026. Oxford PV shipped the world's first commercial perovskite-silicon tandem modules in September 2024, then licensed patents to First Solar (NASDAQ: FSLR) in February 2026 and Trinasolar (exclusive China license). Hanwha Qcells achieved 28.6% tandem cell efficiency using mass-production processes, with commercial production planned for 2026.

First Solar is the best public proxy for perovskite exposure. As the largest U.S. Solar manufacturer with the Oxford PV license, it will likely be the first Western company to mass-produce perovskite-silicon tandems. The efficiency gain from tandems (28-30%+ vs 22-24% for pure silicon) represents a step-function improvement that could reshape the solar industry's cost curve.

Caelux offers a potentially faster route to market with its 'Active Glass' product that converts existing silicon panels to tandem performance without new factories needed. This is a private company but worth monitoring for a future investment route.

Risks center on perovskite durability (degradation over 25+ year module lifetimes remains unproven at scale), Chinese manufacturing dominance in solar, and the fact that First Solar is already a large-cap with the perovskite thesis partially priced in.

name: GMX
one_liner: Sprint 20 REDUCE: Fees declining -48% MoM. Hyperliquid dominance accelerating. Deep value thesis no longer holds at current fee trajectory.
thesis:
GMX is a decentralized perpetual exchange on Arbitrum and Avalanche that pioneered the GLP liquidity pool model. Sprint 19 upgraded GMX to score 86 based on TVL growth and smart money signals.

Sprint 20 reverses that call. Protocol fees have declined -48% month-over-month, a severe deterioration that undermines the core thesis of 'revenue-generating perp DEX at deep discount.' The revenue is no longer generating at the rate that justified the score.

Hyperliquid's dominance continues to accelerate, now commanding 70%+ of on-chain perpetual volume. GMX V2's isolated markets have not been enough to stem market share losses. The TVL growth that triggered the Sprint 19 upgrade appears to have been a temporary rotation rather than sustained institutional accumulation.

The protocol's smart contracts remain battle-tested and the Arbitrum system position provides some floor value. However, the -48% MoM fee decline is not a one-week anomaly, it reflects a structural shift in where perp trading volume is concentrating. Status: REDUCE. Re-evaluate if fees stabilize for 30+ days.

name: dogwifhat
one_liner: Solana's leading dog-with-hat meme coin that reached $4.8B peak market cap and successfully crowdfunded a Las Vegas Sphere appearance, demonstrating community coordination strength.
thesis:
dogwifhat ($WIF) is the quintessential Solana-native meme coin, a dog wearing a knitted hat with zero utility, pure community, and an extraordinary run from under $0.01 to over $4.00 in 2024 (a 40,000x return from launch). Its peak market cap of ~$4.8B made it the third-largest meme coin by market cap after DOGE and SHIB at its height. The community successfully raised $700K to display the WIF logo on the Las Vegas Sphere, demonstrating the kind of organized, meme-powered coordination that separates top-tier meme coins from the tens of thousands of failed imitations.

WIF's investment case is purely reflexive and social: it has no smart contracts, no yield, no roadmap, and no utility. What it has is Lindy effect on Solana, it was the first Solana meme coin to break into the top 20 by market cap and remains the clearest benchmark for the Solana meme coin category. When Solana meme coin season returns, WIF tends to outperform because capital aggregates to the most recognizable ticker.

Risk-adjusted, WIF is better than most meme coins because it has proven its ability to attract and retain liquidity across multiple market cycles. The token launched with no presale, no team allocation, and fair distribution, the purist meme coin attributes that maximize community trust. Liquidity on Jupiter and Raydium is deep enough to support institutional-scale positions.

The core risk is meme coin mean reversion: WIF is down 80%+ from all-time highs and could continue declining if Solana system attention shifts to newer memes (BONK, POPCAT, etc.). There is no fundamental floor, the price is entirely sentiment-driven. Position sizing and timing are everything; this is a momentum and narrative play, not a value investment.
catalysts: Solana meme season rotation returning WIF as blue-chip meme benchmark
CEX listings on remaining Tier 1 exchanges amplifying liquidity
Community coordination events (Sphere-style) driving viral attention cycles
risks: No fundamental floor, price entirely sentiment-driven with 80%+ drawdown history
Newer Solana memes (POPCAT, MEW) competing for mindshare and liquidity
Regulatory action on meme coins as securities or gambling instruments

name: SushiSwap
one_liner: Multi-chain DEX with $500M TVL across 35+ chains, generating $15M+ monthly trading fees, featuring lending (Kashi), launchpad (MISO), and concentrated liquidity with Trident V3.
thesis:
SushiSwap is the most multi-chain DEX in DeFi, operating on 35+ networks including Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Fantom, Avalanche, and many more. This broad deployment gives Sushi access to liquidity and trading volume across the entire L1/L2 space, making it the fallback DEX for chains where Uniswap hasn't deployed. When new L1s or L2s launch, Sushi is often among the first DEXs deployed, capturing early liquidity and trading fees.

Sushi's product surface area is broad: the core AMM generates trading fees, Kashi is a lending platform using isolated risk pools (each token pair has its own risk pool, preventing cascade liquidations), MISO is a token launchpad, and Sushi has experimented with limit orders, cross-chain swaps, and an NFT marketplace. This breadth creates multiple revenue touchpoints but also development fragmentation, Sushi has struggled to execute all products simultaneously with limited engineering resources.

The SushiDAO has faced existential governance crises: the 2020 'exit scam' by anonymous founder Chef Nomi (who later returned funds), multiple CEO departures including Joseph Delong and Jared Grey (the latter amid financial mismanagement allegations in 2023), and a reputational crisis around treasury management. Despite these challenges, the protocol has survived and continues to generate real trading volume, a testament to the resilience of DeFi infrastructure once it achieves sufficient liquidity depth.

SUSHI's investment case is distressed-asset contrarian: at 97%+ below all-time highs, with $500M TVL and $15M+ monthly fees, the market cap of $100-200M implies 1-2x P/R on annualized fees. This is among the cheapest DeFi tokens by revenue multiple. The risk is that governance dysfunction continues, team retention is poor, and Uniswap V4 further compresses Sushi's market share. A governance restructuring with credible leadership could be the catalyst for re-rating.
catalysts: Multi-chain deployment positioning Sushi as default DEX on new chain launches
Leadership stabilization and governance restructuring restoring institutional credibility
xSUSHI fee distribution providing yield for long-term token holders
risks: History of governance crises, founder misconduct, and treasury mismanagement creates persistent distrust
Uniswap V4 and concentrated liquidity competitors capturing Sushi's remaining market share
Developer talent retention difficult given reputational damage and token price decline

name: EnergyX
one_liner: Direct lithium extraction via equity crowdfunding at $9.50/share with agreements covering 4 of the top 10 global lithium producers.
thesis:
EnergyX is developing proprietary direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology that could dramatically improve the economics of lithium production for the EV battery supply chain. The company has secured agreements with 4 of the top 10 lithium producers globally and controls over 100,000 acres of Chilean lithium deposits, positioning it at the center of the lithium supply bottleneck that constrains EV manufacturing scale-up.

The company is currently live on DealMaker (invest.energyx.com) at $9.50/share with an implied valuation of approximately $1.92 billion and a $1,000 minimum investment. This equity crowdfunding structure makes it one of the few deep-tech energy plays accessible to non-accredited investors. Battery-grade lithium carbonate has recovered to $21,000-22,000/tonne in China, up 117% year-over-year, and Morgan Stanley forecasts a deficit of 80,000 mt LCE in 2026, which strengthens the demand side of the thesis.

The primary risk is that DLE technology remains unproven at commercial scale, and the company is pre-revenue with a long commercialization timeline. Equity crowdfunding investments carry severe illiquidity. There is no secondary market for these shares. The $1.92B valuation for a pre-revenue company requires significant execution on technology scale-up and commercial partnerships to justify.

100,000+ acres of Chilean lithium deposits under development

one_liner: Acquired Celestial AI for up to $5.5B, providing the best public photonics exposure with revenue ramp expected late 2028.
thesis:
Celestial AI was acquired by Marvell (NASDAQ: MRVL) for up to $5.5B (December 2025). Revenue from the Celestial AI photonic technology is projected to ramp late 2028, with a billion-dollar run rate targeted by 2029. Marvell is now described as 'the best public photonics exposure.'

Marvell is a large-cap semiconductor company (~$60B+ market cap), so the Celestial AI acquisition represents optionality rather than a pure-play bet. The $5.5B acquisition price signals Marvell's conviction in photonic computing's future. For investors who want public market exposure to the photonic computing thesis, Marvell is now the primary vehicle.

The revenue ramp timeline. Late 2028 to billion-dollar run rate by 2029. Means this is a patient bet. The photonic interconnect technology is complementary to Marvell's existing data center networking business, creating natural cross-selling opportunities.

As a large-cap, Marvell offers lower risk than pure-play photonic companies but also lower use to the photonics thesis specifically. This is the conservative way to play the photonic computing transition.

one_liner: Quantum annealer demonstrating 10x faster defense optimization, with a $10M Fortune 100 enterprise deal signed.

D-Wave is the only quantum company with a commercially available annealing system and is demonstrating 10x faster solutions for defense optimization problems. FY2025 revenue hit $24.6M (+179% growth), with $32.8M in post-earnings bookings in Jan-Feb 2026.

The $10M Fortune 100 QCaaS (Quantum Computing as a Service) agreement validates enterprise willingness to pay for quantum optimization. D-Wave's annealing approach is optimized for a specific class of problems (combinatorial optimization) where it already shows practical advantage.

Weakness. Quantum annealing is a different approach from gate-based quantum computing and faces skepticism about long-term utility. Revenue is still small relative to the ~$3B valuation. The distinction between quantum advantage and classical optimization algorithms remains debated in the technical community.

name: Voltair (YC W2026)
one_liner: YC W2026 standout building self-charging drones that perch on power lines. Solving the range limitation that constraints all drone operations.
thesis:
Voltair is a YC W2026 standout developing self-charging drones that perch on power lines to recharge. This addresses the single biggest constraint in drone operations. Limited flight time (typically 20-40 minutes for commercial drones). If drones can autonomously land on power lines and recharge, the operational range becomes essentially unlimited.

The concept is elegant in its simplicity. Existing power line infrastructure becomes a universal drone charging network at zero additional cost. This could enable persistent drone operations for infrastructure inspection, delivery, surveillance, and agriculture without dedicated charging infrastructure.

The intersection of autonomous drones and energy harvesting is a natural YC bet. Combining hardware innovation with a clear path to recurring revenue (drone-as-a-service for utility inspection, etc.).

Risks are significant. Perching on live power lines presents enormous safety and regulatory challenges, utility companies may resist unauthorized use of their infrastructure, and the technology must work reliably in all weather conditions. This is an early-stage, high-concept company with significant technical hurdles remaining.

name: Opulous
one_liner: Lowest market cap liquid music royalty token offering DeFi loans backed by music royalties on Algorand.
thesis:
Opulous occupies a unique niche at the intersection of music industry IP and DeFi. The protocol offers loans backed by music royalties, creating a novel financial primitive that connects the $30 billion+ annual global music industry to on-chain capital markets. The $OPUL token trades in the $0.06-0.18 range, making it the lowest market cap liquid token in the music royalty tokenization category. A sector where the gap between total addressable market and current tokenized assets is 600-3,000x.

The live product with functioning royalty-backed loans demonstrates genuine product-market fit at small scale. Unlike many DeFi protocols that rely on token emissions for yield, Opulous generates real economic activity by connecting music IP holders with capital providers. This creates a more sustainable economic model than pure speculation, though the absolute scale remains tiny relative to both the music industry and the broader DeFi system.

The Algorand system positioning is both a differentiator and a limitation. Algorand provides low fees and fast finality suitable for royalty micropayments, but the chain's limited DeFi activity and smaller developer community restrict Opulous's ability to integrate with broader DeFi infrastructure. Cross-chain expansion or integration with larger ecosystems would significantly improve the protocol's addressable market.

The core risk is that music royalty tokenization may remain a niche forever. The music industry is notoriously slow to adopt new technologies, and the legal complexity of royalty ownership across jurisdictions creates significant regulatory overhead. Without a breakout use case or major label partnership, Opulous risks staying permanently small.

Major label or distributor partnership would validate royalty tokenization model
Cross-chain expansion beyond Algorand to Ethereum or Solana would increase DeFi composability
risks: Tiny market cap with low liquidity. Potential for severe price impact on any significant trade
Algorand system has limited DeFi activity, constraining composability and integration
Music industry adoption of tokenization is unproven at scale. Legal and regulatory complexity is high

name: Fourth Power
one_liner: MIT/Georgia Tech spinout building molten carbon thermal storage backed by Breakthrough Energy Ventures and Munich Re.
thesis:
Fourth Power is an MIT and Georgia Tech spinout developing a novel molten carbon thermal energy storage system. The company has raised between $19M (per Asymmetric Opportunity Atlas) and $39M (per 1000x Opportunity Map) in Series A funding, backed by Breakthrough Energy Ventures (BEV, Bill Gates' climate fund) and Munich Re, one of the world's largest reinsurers. The technology stores energy by heating materials to extreme temperatures, targeting deployment costs below lithium-ion by 2028.

The company is currently at TRL 5-6, with a 1 MWh prototype under construction near Boston and a demonstration expected later in 2026. Full-scale 100 MWh systems are targeted for 2028. The backing from BEV and Munich Re is notable. BEV has a strong track record of identifying breakthrough clean energy technologies, and Munich Re's involvement suggests the technology has passed rigorous risk assessment by a major insurer.

The primary risk is the early-stage nature of the technology. At TRL 5-6, Fourth Power is still in the prototype phase with no commercial revenue and a long timeline to deployment. There is no public investment mechanism available, making this accessible only to institutional or accredited investors in future rounds. The energy storage space has a high failure rate. As Ambri's Chapter 11 bankruptcy demonstrates, the gap between promising prototypes and viable factories can be fatal.

Full-scale 100 MWh systems targeted for deployment in 2028
BEV and Munich Re backing validates technology and de-risks future funding rounds

name: Farcaster
one_liner: Decentralized social protocol acquired by Neynar for $180M refund. No official token coming, but $DEGEN community token provides system exposure.
thesis:
Farcaster was one of the most anticipated airdrop opportunities in crypto, having raised $180M in VC funding and built a decentralized social protocol with 40-60K daily active users. However, in January 2026, Neynar acquired the Farcaster protocol, and Merkle Manufactory announced it would repay the $180M in VC funding. Dan Romero stated explicitly. 'I had wanted to issue a token for Farcaster, but the right timing never came.' No official token exists and no airdrop is coming.

This fundamental shift in the Farcaster narrative changes the investment thesis entirely. The protocol's genuine Power Badge daily active users were only approximately 4,360, far below the headline 40-60K DAU figures. Lifetime protocol revenue reached $2.8M. Meaningful for a decentralized social protocol but modest in absolute terms. The Neynar acquisition suggests the founders concluded that the protocol's value was better captured through a corporate acquisition than through tokenization.

The $DEGEN community token, created organically by the Farcaster community rather than the protocol team, provides the only token-based exposure to the Farcaster system. However, $DEGEN is community-created and has no official backing from the protocol or Neynar. Its value is entirely dependent on the continued engagement of the Farcaster community, which faces headwinds from the loss of token-incentive expectations.

Farcaster remains a technically impressive decentralized social protocol, but the investment thesis has been fundamentally damaged by the acquisition and token cancellation. The builder community on Warpcast retains value as an information source and networking tool, but the financial upside that drove much of the user acquisition is gone.

name: ispace
one_liner: Closest to lunar hardware deployment of any public company, but two crash landings make this a high-risk bet on the third attempt.
thesis:
ispace (TSE: 9348) trades at ~500 yen with a ~$450M market cap and 4.7B yen in revenue. The company is the closest to operational lunar hardware of any publicly traded company, focusing on lunar landers and rovers for commercial and government customers.

The critical risk is the track record. Two crash landings occurred. The first mission (M1) crashed on the Moon's surface in April 2023, and the second mission also experienced issues. These failures demonstrate both the difficulty of lunar landing and the execution risk inherent in the company. However, each failure provides engineering data that improves subsequent attempts.

The lunar economy thesis is compelling. NASA's Artemis program, commercial lunar payload services (CLPS), and growing interest in lunar resources create a demand backdrop. Ispace's Japanese origin also positions it for JAXA contracts and Asian space market participation.

At $450M market cap with real revenue but a track record of mission failures, this is a high-risk bet that the engineering team can convert accumulated learning into a successful mission. A successful third landing would dramatically re-rate the stock.

name: Zora Attention Markets
one_liner: Attention Markets launched February 2026 on Solana where users trade tokens tied to viral memes, hashtags, and cultural trends.
thesis:
Zora's Attention Markets represent perhaps the most experimental new market structure in crypto. The ability to trade tokens tied to cultural phenomena. Viral memes, hashtags, and trending topics. Launched on Solana in February 2026, this product extends Zora's evolution from an NFT platform into a broader attention economy protocol. The $ZORA token provides the earliest-stage pure exposure to the attention market thesis.

The concept sits squarely in the 'toy phase'. Trading tokens tied to memes and cultural trends sounds absurd to most serious investors. This is precisely the signal that historical pattern analysis suggests is most bullish for model-shifting opportunities. Every previous crypto explosion (Bitcoin, NFTs, DeFi) was initially dismissed as trivially stupid before becoming enormously valuable. Whether attention markets follow this path or remain a novelty is the central question.

Zora has built credibility through its NFT-era infrastructure and creator community, which provides a foundation of users who understand digital cultural assets. The Solana launch provides low-cost, high-speed transactions suitable for the rapid trading that attention markets require. The attention economy thesis is backed by the reality that human attention is increasingly the scarcest and most valuable resource in the digital economy.

The risks are substantial. Attention markets are inherently speculative and may face regulatory scrutiny as a form of event or prediction trading. The lack of specific backer data for the attention markets product (as opposed to Zora's broader NFT business) suggests this is an experimental extension rather than a heavily capitalized new venture. The February 2026 launch means the product is only months old with no established track record.
catalysts: Attention economy thesis gaining traction as cultural capital becomes quantifiable on-chain
Solana system growth and meme culture creating natural user acquisition funnel
Zora's existing NFT creator community provides initial adoption base for attention market products
risks: Highly speculative attention economy model with no proven sustainable business model
Regulatory risk. Trading tokens tied to cultural trends may face securities or gambling classification
Product launched February 2026. Only months of track record with no established performance data

name: Stardust Solutions
one_liner: Largest geoengineering round ($75M Series A) for stratospheric aerosol injection, with extreme ethical and regulatory risk.
thesis:
Stardust Solutions raised the largest-ever geoengineering round. A $75M Series A led by Lowercarbon Capital (Chris Sacca) in October 2025. The US-Israeli company plans stratospheric aerosol injection using proprietary reflective particles (not sulfur dioxide, which has known ozone-depletion risks). Outdoor experiments are planned from April 2026.

The thesis is binary and extreme. If climate change accelerates faster than emissions reduction, solar radiation management (SRM) may become politically necessary regardless of ethical objections. Stardust is positioning to be the leading commercial provider of a technology that governments may eventually mandate. The $75M from Chris Sacca's Lowercarbon Capital provides credibility in the climate-tech investment community.

However, the ethical and regulatory risks are unprecedented. Geoengineering is banned in Mexico, multiple US states are proposing bans, and the scientific community is deeply divided on whether stratospheric aerosol injection should be pursued at all. Termination shock (the rapid warming that occurs if injection stops) creates a commitment trap. International governance frameworks do not exist.

This is the most controversial entry in the entire report. It is included because the capital deployment is real and the smart-money signal (Sacca) is strong, but the ethical and regulatory risks make this unsuitable for most investors.

If climate emergency accelerates, SRM may become politically necessary

name: Axie Infinity
one_liner: The original P2E gaming pioneer that reached 2.7M daily active users and $1.3B monthly revenue in 2021, now rebuilding on Ronin with Axie Origins and a treasury war chest of $150M+.
thesis:
Axie Infinity represents both the greatest success and cautionary tale in Web3 gaming. At its 2021 peak, it had 2.7M daily active users, $1.3B monthly revenue, and spawned an entire economy of Axie scholars (players from developing countries earning income by playing). The subsequent collapse of the SLP (Smooth Love Potion) play-to-earn economy and the $625M Ronin bridge hack in March 2022 (the largest crypto hack by dollar value at the time) devastated the project.

Sky Mavis (the developer) has spent 2022-2025 rebuilding: the Ronin bridge hack was fully repaid using company treasury and Binance support, Axie Origins was launched as a redesigned game without pay-to-earn death spirals, and the Ronin blockchain has been rebuilt with improved security including more validators and institutional node operators. The Ronin system now hosts multiple games beyond Axie, including Pixels (which briefly reached 1M+ daily active users in early 2024), demonstrating that Ronin has genuine gaming system potential beyond Axie itself.

AXS treasury governance controls significant assets accumulated during the 2021 bull run. The AXS staking contract holds millions of tokens, and the DAO treasury has $150M+ in assets. This provides a significant runway for continued development without needing external fundraising. AXS stakers earn fees from Axie marketplace trading, creating a genuine yield mechanism.

The recovery thesis is long-dated: Sky Mavis needs to rebuild daily active user count from 50-100K back toward millions. Axie Origins' improved game design and removal of the most extractive P2E mechanics is a positive signal. The Ronin chain's success with non-Axie games (Pixels, Apeiron) suggests the platform has multi-game legs. However, the reputational overhang from the hack and P2E collapse will take multiple years and sustained success to overcome.
catalysts: Ronin blockchain system expansion beyond Axie showing multi-game platform potential
Axie Origins improved game mechanics rebuilding sustainable player economy
AXS staking yield attracting long-term holders as treasury generates marketplace fees
risks: Ronin bridge hack reputational damage with $625M loss remains in institutional memory
P2E economic model collapse may be irreversible regardless of game design improvements
Daily active users still 95%+ below 2021 peak with limited signs of recovery

name: Rejuvenate Bio
one_liner: George Church's gene therapy longevity company with active Wefunder campaign at $59.4M valuation, but employee count dropped 67% to ~2 people.
thesis:
Rejuvenate Bio is George Church's gene therapy company targeting aging through delivery of longevity genes (FGF21, sTGFbR, alpha-Klotho) via gene therapy vectors. The company has an active Wefunder campaign (Reg CF, ~$100 minimum) at a $59.4M valuation, making it one of the few longevity companies accessible to non-accredited investors.

The science is compelling. Church is one of the world's foremost geneticists, and the target genes have strong preclinical evidence for lifespan extension in animal models takes a gene therapy approach, delivering multiple longevity factors simultaneously. Is fundamentally different from supplement-based approaches and could deliver step-function improvements in healthspan.

However, the red flags are significant. Employee count dropped 67% from 2023 to 2024, leaving approximately 2 employees. This is a company in survival mode, not growth mode. The $59.4M valuation on a Wefunder campaign with minimal staff suggests the brand name (George Church) is doing most of the heavy lifting. This carries extremely high risk and is a binary bet on whether the company can raise enough to continue operations and advance to meaningful preclinical milestones.

George Church's reputation provides scientific credibility and fundraising ability
Gene therapy approach could deliver step-function longevity improvements vs supplements
risks: Employee count dropped 67% to ~2 people. Company in survival mode
Reg CF crowdfunding at $59.4M valuation with minimal operational capacity
Gene therapy delivery and safety hurdles remain unsolved for longevity applications

name: Ginkgo Bioworks
one_liner: The synbio platform company pivoting to autonomous labs, trading at ~$6.80-9.71 with $421-552M market cap, burning $125-150M/year with $423M cash.
thesis:
Ginkgo Bioworks (NYSE: DNA) trades at ~$6.80-9.71, with a ~$421-552M market cap with revenue declining ($170M in FY2025, down 25%), but the company is pivoting to autonomous labs with a $47M government contract. It is burning $125-150M/year with $423M cash remaining, implying roughly 3 years of runway at current burn.

Analyst consensus is bearish. 1 Buy vs. 3 Sell recommendations. This is a contrarian bet on the platform model surviving the current synbio downturn. Ginkgo's 'cell programming' platform approach is differentiated. It doesn't make products itself but engineers organisms for partners. But the declining revenue suggests the platform hasn't achieved product-market fit at scale.

The $47M government contract and pivot to autonomous labs represent potential catalysts, but the declining topline and negative sentiment make this high-risk. At $421-552M market cap with $423M cash, the market is essentially valuing the operating business near zero.

This is a contrarian deep value play on the synbio sector recovering and Ginkgo's platform model surviving.

Platform model could benefit from sector recovery if synbio sentiment improves

name: Multipli.fi
one_liner: DeFi yield aggregation protocol offering automated multi-strategy vaults for optimized returns across lending and liquidity markets.
thesis:
Multipli.fi is a DeFi yield aggregation protocol that provides automated multi-strategy vaults, routing capital across lending markets and liquidity pools for optimized returns. The protocol targets the growing demand for passive DeFi yield without the complexity of manual strategy management.

Currently tokenless and in early stages. The yield aggregation space is competitive with established players like Yearn, Convex, and Concrete, but new entrants with superior routing algorithms or novel strategy sets can carve out meaningful market share.

Key monitoring points: TVL traction, strategy performance vs. competitors, team doxxing, VC announcements, and any points/rewards programs.

Growing demand for automated DeFi yield management as retail users seek passive returns
Novel strategy routing could differentiate from established yield aggregators
risks: Highly competitive yield aggregation market with established players (Yearn, Convex)

name: FLOKI
one_liner: Elon Musk's dog-inspired meme coin evolving into a utility system with Valhalla play-to-earn game, FlokiFi DeFi suite, and TokenFi RWA tokenization platform.
thesis:
FLOKI started as a pure Elon Musk dog-meme play (named after his Shiba Inu) but has executed the most ambitious pivot of any meme coin toward genuine utility. The project now comprises three distinct products: Valhalla (a play-to-earn metaverse game), FlokiFi (a DeFi suite including locker and staking tools), and TokenFi (a no-code platform for tokenizing real-world assets). This utility-building ambition separates FLOKI from pure meme coins and justifies a higher tier assessment than WIF or BONK.

TokenFi in particular has strategic merit: it targets the RWA tokenization market with a no-code interface, allowing non-technical issuers to tokenize assets without smart contract expertise. As the RWA market grows (BCG estimates $16T tokenized by 2030), TokenFi's position as an accessible on-ramp has real product value. The platform has processed $50M+ in tokenized assets since launch and has partnerships with real estate firms and fund administrators.

Valhalla's P2E game faces the perennial challenge of crypto gaming, token emission creates sell pressure, and most P2E games collapse when new player inflows slow. FLOKI's marketing spend has been aggressive (London Underground ads, Premier League partnerships) and has built brand awareness beyond the crypto-native audience, but converting brand awareness to sustainable token demand remains unproven.

FLOKI trades on BNB Chain and Ethereum simultaneously, with BNB Chain being its primary liquidity venue. Token burns through the Tax Authority mechanism have removed trillions of FLOKI from supply, reducing inflation. The primary risks are the typical meme coin traps: if Valhalla or TokenFi fail to achieve escape velocity, FLOKI reverts to pure speculative memecoin dynamics with no fundamental support.
catalysts: TokenFi RWA platform gaining traction as no-code tokenization layer
Valhalla game launch on major mobile app stores driving retail user acquisition
Elon Musk social media engagement historically spiking FLOKI price 50-200%
risks: P2E game economics historically collapse when new player growth slows
Utility layer revenue not yet sufficient to provide fundamental price support
Token exists simultaneously on Ethereum and BNB Chain creating fragmented liquidity

name: Stake DAO
one_liner: DeFi yield optimizer with $119M TVL at $7.8M market cap (15x TVL/MCap). Specializes in Curve and Balancer meta-governance strategies. WARNING: TVL declined 22% in past 7 days.
thesis:
Stake DAO is a non-custodial yield optimization platform that aggregates DeFi yields through strategies built on top of Curve, Balancer, Frax, and other major protocols. The platform manages $161M in TVL across its various vaults and liquid lockers.

Stake DAO's key differentiator is its meta-governance approach. By accumulating CRV, BAL, and FXS tokens through its liquid lockers, Stake DAO wields significant voting power in Curve and Balancer governance, which it uses to direct emissions to its own pools, boosting yields for depositors. This creates a flywheel: more deposits lead to more governance power, which leads to higher yields, which attracts more deposits.

At $7.8M market cap against $161M TVL, the 20x TVL/MCap ratio suggests meaningful undervaluation. The SDT token captures platform fees (15% performance fee) and governs the protocol's gauge-voting strategy.

The protocol faces headwinds from the broader Curve system slowdown and competition from Convex and Yearn. However, its multi-protocol approach (not just Curve) provides diversification that competitors lack. The bull case requires a Curve system renaissance and continued DeFi yield demand.
catalysts: Curve gauge voting power increasing SDT yield advantage
Expansion to new protocols beyond Curve system
DeFi yield demand recovery in alt season
risks: Curve system decline reducing gauge voting value
Competition from Convex and Yearn V3
Low trading liquidity for SDT token

name: Human Archive (YC W2026)
one_liner: YC W2026 standout archiving the physical world for embodied AI. Creating the spatial data layer for robot navigation and interaction.
thesis:
Human Archive is a YC W2026 standout focused on archiving the physical world for embodied AI. The concept is creating a full spatial data layer that robots and AI agents can use to navigate and interact with real-world environments.

As embodied AI moves from labs to deployment (warehouses, homes, cities), AI systems need detailed maps and models of physical spaces. Human Archive is building this data infrastructure layer. Analogous to how Google Maps created the spatial data layer for mobile applications.

The timing aligns with the broader embodied AI wave. Tesla Optimus, Figure AI, 1X, and others are all approaching commercial deployment, creating demand for spatial data that doesn't currently exist at scale.

Risks are typical of very early-stage ventures. The concept is compelling but execution is unproven, the market for spatial data for robotics may take years to develop, and competing approaches (real-time SLAM, simulation-based training) may reduce the need for pre-built spatial archives. Private with no public investment route.
catalysts: YC W2026 backing. Accelerator validation of spatial data for embodied AI
Embodied AI companies approaching commercial deployment creating data demand
Building the 'Google Maps of physical spaces' for robot navigation
risks: Very early-stage. Concept compelling but execution unproven
Market for spatial robotics data may take years to develop meaningful revenue
Competing approaches (real-time SLAM, simulation) may reduce need for pre-built archives

name: Biomemory
one_liner: Paris-based DNA storage company that acquired Catalog Technologies in March 2026, creating the largest combined DNA storage IP portfolio.
thesis:
Biomemory (Paris) acquired Catalog Technologies (March 2026), creating the largest combined DNA storage IP portfolio. The acquisition consolidates the DNA storage sector, positioning Biomemory alongside Atlas Data Storage (Twist spinoff) as one of two leading players.

DNA data storage is a frontier technology that addresses the long-term mismatch between exponential data creation and linear storage capacity growth. DNA can theoretically store exabytes per gram and last thousands of years. The Catalog Technologies acquisition brings additional IP and engineering talent.

However, Biomemory is significantly less well-funded than Atlas Data Storage ($155M seed). The Paris base may also limit access to Silicon Valley talent and capital networks. DNA storage remains pre-commercial with a 5-10+ year timeline to meaningful revenue.

No retail access. Private company with limited public information about funding status. The Catalog acquisition is a consolidation signal that suggests the sector is maturing from pure research to early commercialization efforts.

name: USA Rare Earth
one_liner: Earlier-stage rare earth play with $3.1B government capital package, offering higher-risk higher-reward exposure vs MP Materials.
thesis:
USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) is an earlier-stage play on the American rare earth independence thesis. The company has secured a $3.1B government capital package, signaling significant federal backing for domestic rare earth development.

While MP Materials is the established leader, USAR offers a higher-risk, higher-reward profile for investors who believe the rare earth independence thesis will require multiple domestic producers to meet demand. The government capital package provides a funding runway that most junior miners lack.

The investment case depends heavily on execution. Transitioning from government-backed development stage to actual production. The rare earth processing chain (mining, separation, oxide production, metal making, magnet manufacturing) is technically demanding, and few Western companies have mastered the full chain.

Risks include that the company is much earlier-stage than MP Materials, government funding programs are subject to political winds, and the rare earth market is structurally challenging for high-cost Western producers competing against Chinese scale.

Federal rare earth independence policy creating structural demand for domestic producers
Higher-beta alternative to MP Materials for rare earth exposure

Rare earth processing chain technically demanding. Few Western companies have mastered it

name: NuScale Power
one_liner: First NRC-certified small modular reactor design, partnering for 6GW U.S. Deployment.
thesis:
NuScale Power holds a unique regulatory moat. It is the only company with a Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) certified small modular reactor (SMR) design. In an industry where regulatory approval can take a decade and cost hundreds of millions, this certification is an extraordinarily valuable asset. The ENTRA1 partnership targets 6GW of capacity across 6 plants (72 modules), representing the largest new nuclear program in the United States in decades.

The company's $1.3B in available liquidity provides sufficient runway to reach deployment milestones. The stock's 80% decline from its peak creates potential value if execution improves, as the NRC certification and ENTRA1 partnership provide a real foundation for recovery. The growing demand for clean, reliable baseload power from AI data centers adds a secular demand tailwind.

However, the risks are substantial. No operational SMR plants exist anywhere in the U.S., and NuScale's annual revenue is only $31.5M, making this fundamentally a pre-revenue technology company. First deployment is not expected until the early 2030s, requiring years of patient capital. The previous CFPP project cancellation in Idaho raised serious questions about cost competitiveness. At approximately $2.5B market cap for a pre-revenue company with deployment years away, NuScale trades primarily on narrative and optionality rather than fundamentals.

ENTRA1 partnership targeting 6GW across 6 plants (72 modules)

Revenue only $31.5M, first deployment not until early 2030s

name: Royal.io
one_liner: Music royalty trading platform with $7.5M total volume and $156K in actual royalties paid out to token holders.
thesis:
Royal.io stands out in the music royalty tokenization space for one critical reason. It has actually paid royalties to token holders. With $156K in royalties distributed and $7.5M in total trading volume, Royal.io has demonstrated the complete lifecycle of music royalty tokenization. From initial offering to secondary trading to actual royalty distribution. This end-to-end execution is rare in a sector full of promises but short on delivery.

The platform allows users to purchase fractional ownership of music royalties, creating a new asset class that provides exposure to the economics of individual songs or catalogs. The $7.5M in cumulative trading volume indicates genuine market demand, though the absolute scale remains small. New products are reportedly in development, which could expand the platform's capabilities and attract a broader user base.

The lack of a token is both a risk and an opportunity. Without a native token, there is no direct way to invest in Royal.io's platform growth. Users can only buy individual song royalty tokens. However, a future token launch or equity raise could provide broader exposure to the platform's success. The company's focus on building a real product rather than launching a speculative token is arguably a positive signal in a sector plagued by vaporware.

The primary limitation is scale. $7.5M in total trading volume and $156K in royalties paid are meaningful proofs of concept but tiny relative to the opportunity. Music royalty tokenization needs a breakout moment. Perhaps a major label partnership or a viral artist drop. To transition from niche experiment to mainstream financial product.

Potential token launch would create direct platform-level investment exposure
Major artist or label partnership would provide mainstream validation and volume growth

name: ValleyDAO
one_liner: Synthetic biology BioDAO with Imperial College London partnership, trading at $843K market cap after a 97% decline.
thesis:
ValleyDAO is a BioDAO within the Bio Protocol system focused on synthetic biology. The engineering of biological systems for useful purposes including sustainable materials, biofuels, and novel therapeutics. The DAO has established a partnership with Imperial College London, one of the world's leading research universities, lending institutional credibility to its scientific mission.

GROW trades at $0.076, down 97% from ATH, with a market cap of approximately $843K. Like CryoDAO, this places ValleyDAO in the micro-cap dead zone where liquidity is negligible and price discovery is unreliable. The Imperial College partnership is a genuine differentiator. Access to world-class synthetic biology research infrastructure and talent is valuable, even if the market currently assigns almost no value to it.

Synthetic biology is one of the fastest-growing fields in the life sciences, with the global market projected to reach $65B+ by 2030. The potential for DeSci to fund and accelerate synthetic biology research is compelling in theory. Traditional funding for SynBio startups is heavily concentrated in the US and UK, leaving many researchers in emerging markets without access to capital.

However, the practical reality is challenging. At $843K market cap, ValleyDAO lacks the resources to fund meaningful synthetic biology research programs, which typically require millions in lab equipment, reagents, and researcher compensation. Recovery requires either a massive DeSci sector revival or a specific breakthrough from the Imperial College partnership.

Synthetic biology sector growth ($65B+ by 2030) raising awareness of DeSci funding models
Bio Protocol system revival lifting all constituent BioDAOs

97% decline from ATH suggests potential permanent capital destruction
Synthetic biology research requires expensive lab infrastructure that a micro-cap DAO cannot fund

name: FinalSpark
one_liner: Runs the world's first online neuroplatform. 16 brain organoids with ~160,000 neurons accessible remotely via Python API.
thesis:
FinalSpark (Switzerland) runs the world's first online neuroplatform. 16 brain organoids with approximately 160,000 neurons accessible remotely via Python API. They can reliably store 1 bit of information per organoid. The company is self-funded (~CHF 1M from founders' prior exit) and seeking CHF 50M for next-stage development.

Ten universities have access including Michigan, Exeter, and Free University of Berlin. The academic validation provides credibility, but the technology is extremely early-stage. Storing 1 bit per organoid is a proof of concept, not a product.

FinalSpark sits in the same biological computing model as Cortical Labs but at an earlier stage and with significantly less funding. The energy advantage of biological computing is real. Neurons consume less power than a handheld calculator. But the path from 160K neurons to commercial-scale computing is long and uncertain.

No retail access. Self-funded with minimal external capital. Watch for the CHF 50M raise as a validation event.

name: Paradromics
one_liner: Record-breaking 200+ bits-per-second BCI data rate achiever, accessible only to accredited investors on secondary markets.
thesis:
Paradromics has achieved a record-breaking data rate of 200+ bits per second (bps) for brain-computer interface communications, establishing a technical milestone that no other BCI company has matched. This high-bandwidth approach targets the most data-intensive BCI applications, including full communication restoration for paralyzed patients and eventually more ambitious human-computer interaction paradigms.

The company operates in the rapidly growing BCI sector where total funding tripled to $867M in 2025. Paradromics represents the highest-bandwidth approach among BCI companies, technically differentiated from Synchron's lower-bandwidth but less invasive endovascular approach and positioned alongside Neuralink's direct cortical implant strategy. Access is limited to accredited investors via secondary markets.

The primary challenge is that Paradromics has significantly less public information available compared to Synchron and Neuralink. No specific funding amounts or named backers appear in the source documents, which limits the ability to assess financial runway and institutional validation. The company is very early stage with no FDA-authorized clinical trials mentioned, and the BCI market faces fundamental questions about commercial viability and regulatory timelines. This is a high-conviction technology bet with limited visibility into execution progress.

name: Gala Games
one_liner: Blockchain gaming system with 50+ games including Mirandus and Spider Tanks, powered by a node network of 50,000+ operator nodes creating decentralized game distribution.
thesis:
Gala Games entered the Web3 gaming space with an ambitious vision: a decentralized gaming platform where players own in-game assets and participate in game governance via node ownership. The Gala node network (50,000+ operator nodes at peak) was designed to distribute game content and earn GALA rewards, creating a Bittorrent-for-gaming model. Node licenses sold for thousands of dollars each, generating significant early revenue for the system.

The game library has expanded to 50+ titles in various development stages, including Mirandus (fantasy RPG), Spider Tanks (battle arena), and Legacy (city builder). The acquisition of mobile game studio Certain Affinity and strategic partnerships with established game developers signals an attempt to bridge Web2 gaming quality with Web3 ownership. Town Star, a farming simulation game, has seen consistent play since launch, proving that Gala can sustain at least some games post-launch hype cycle.

However, Gala has been plagued by internal disputes and governance challenges. A 2022 incident where Gala's co-founder Eric Schiermeyer accused co-founder Wright Thurston of stealing $130M+ in company funds resulted in litigation that was eventually settled. This reputational damage, combined with the collapse of most P2E economies following Axie Infinity's decline, severely impacted Gala's narrative and token price. GALA is down 95%+ from all-time highs.

The recovery thesis requires Web3 gaming to achieve a second wave of mainstream adoption, with Gala's broad game library serving as a diversified bet across gaming genres. The massive GALA supply (69B+ circulating of 35B max, after a controversial token restructuring in 2023) creates extreme dilution risk. The node network also creates sell pressure as node operators earn and dump GALA rewards. This is a high-risk speculative position requiring a conviction call on Web3 gaming broadly.

Web3 gaming second wave if P2E economics are redesigned for sustainability
Node network restructuring improving tokenomics and reducing sell pressure

Token supply extremely large and node operator rewards create constant sell pressure
Most P2E games in portfolio have failed to achieve sustainable player economies

name: The Sandbox
one_liner: Voxel-based metaverse platform with 200+ brand partnerships including Adidas, Snoop Dogg, and Warner Music, featuring user-generated content tools and 166,464 LAND parcels.
thesis:
The Sandbox is the most brand-partnered metaverse project in crypto, with 200+ partnerships including Adidas, Snoop Dogg, The Walking Dead, Warner Music Group, Atari, and dozens of premium IPs. This partnership portfolio was assembled during the 2021 metaverse mania when brands were desperate to establish virtual land presences, creating a situation where The Sandbox had the brand relationships but needed the users to make them meaningful.

The core product is a Minecraft/Roblox-inspired voxel world where LAND owners (166,464 total parcels) can build experiences using the VoxEdit and Game Maker tools. The user-generated content model is sound in concept, Roblox proved that user-created games can scale to hundreds of millions of users. However, Sandbox's transition from brand partnerships to organic creator system has been slower than projected, with active daily users in the tens of thousands rather than millions.

SAND is the system currency used for LAND purchases, game experiences, and governance voting. The LAND NFT market peaked at extraordinary valuations (individual parcels selling for $400K+) and has collapsed 95%+, dragging SAND price with it. The virtual real estate narrative that drove the 2021 boom has been thoroughly deflated, making recovery dependent on rebuilding on actual user engagement metrics rather than speculation.

The investment case at current prices is a long-dated option on metaverse gaming adoption. The Sandbox has genuine brand relationships, working technology, and a creator SDK that is more polished than most competitors. If virtual worlds achieve mainstream adoption in the next 5-10 years, The Sandbox's existing infrastructure and partnerships provide a meaningful head start. But this requires patience measured in years and tolerance for potentially further declines if the metaverse narrative remains out of favor.

VoxEdit creator tooling maturation attracting independent game developers
Metaverse narrative revival if AR/VR hardware adoption accelerates
risks: Metaverse narrative severely damaged post-2021 with no clear catalyst for revival
Virtual land speculative premium has collapsed and may not recover this cycle
Decentraland and other metaverse competitors competing for same limited user base

name: Koniku
one_liner: Builds 'smell cyborgs' fusing 128 biological neurons with silicon for volatile organic compound detection, with an $8M Airbus contract at SFO.
thesis:
Koniku (San Rafael, CA) builds 'smell cyborgs' fusing biological neurons with silicon. 128 active neurons detecting volatile organic compounds. They claim an $8M Airbus contract for aviation threat detection deployed at SFO, though scientific skepticism is significant and employee count has declined.

The use case is compelling. Biological neurons as sensors for threat detection in airports and security settings. But the company has struggled with execution. The decline in employee count is a red flag, suggesting either funding difficulties or a pivot.

Koniku represents a niche application of biological computing focused on biosensing rather than general computation. If the Airbus deployment proves successful at scale, it could validate the neuron-on-silicon approach for defense and security applications.

No retail access. Private company with limited public information about current funding status.
catalysts: Claimed $8M Airbus contract for aviation threat detection at SFO
Biological neuron sensing approach could extend to defense and bioterrorism detection
Growing broader interest in biological computing from DARPA and NSF
risks: Significant scientific skepticism about the technology's actual capabilities
Employee count has declined. Potential execution or funding issues
Only 128 neurons. Extremely small scale with unclear path to commercial viability

name: Decentraland
one_liner: Fully decentralized metaverse governed by DAO with 90,601 LAND parcels, hosting live events for Sotheby's, Samsung, and Atari with 60,000+ monthly active users.
thesis:
Decentraland is the most decentralized virtual world in crypto, fully governed by its DAO since 2020, with the founding team having no special authority over the protocol. This ideological purity is both its greatest strength (genuine community ownership) and a meaningful weakness (slower decision-making, less capacity to pivot quickly vs. centralized competitors). The 90,601 LAND parcels create a fixed virtual real estate map where location and adjacency create scarcity dynamics.

The protocol has hosted genuinely notable real-world brand events: Sotheby's opened a virtual auction house, Samsung held a virtual launch event, Atari opened a virtual casino, and Decentraland hosts a biannual Metaverse Fashion Week. These events demonstrate that large brands take Decentraland seriously as a venue for digital brand activations. Monthly active users fluctuate between 50,000-100,000, modest by gaming standards but real engagement rather than speculative.

MANA is used for LAND purchases, wearable NFTs, and in-world transactions. The DAO treasury holds significant MANA and stablecoin reserves accumulated from LAND sales, providing multi-year development runway even without external funding. The Foundation's grant program funds community-built experiences, creating an organic creator system. Wearable NFT sales and the Avatar marketplace create ongoing economic activity denominated in MANA.

The core challenge is that Decentraland's concurrent user count peaked at ~6,000 users simultaneously during major events, a fraction of mainstream gaming titles. The browser-based 3D engine creates performance limitations that prevent the immersive experience needed to retain casual users. The decentralized governance model, while ideologically pure, has slowed adaptation to competitive pressures from The Sandbox and emerging AI-powered virtual worlds. MANA is a speculative long on metaverse adoption with real but limited current utility.
catalysts: Metaverse Fashion Week and brand event calendar driving periodic user spikes
DAO grants funding new immersive experiences and creator tooling
Mobile client development potentially expanding accessible user base beyond desktop
risks: Concurrent user count significantly below sustainable virtual world thresholds
DAO governance slows product iteration vs. centralized gaming competitors
3D engine limitations create poor user experience compared to mainstream gaming standards

name: Agronomics
one_liner: A listed fund on the LSE holding shares in Galy and other cellular agriculture companies. The simplest retail exposure to lab-grown materials.
thesis:
Agronomics Ltd (LSE: ANIC) is a listed fund holding shares in Galy and other cellular agriculture companies. It provides the simplest retail exposure to the lab-grown materials thesis. Investors can buy a single stock and get diversified exposure to a basket of private cellular agriculture companies.

The fund structure means Agronomics rises and falls with the sector. Its key holding in Galy (lab-grown cotton, $33M Series B led by Breakthrough Energy Ventures) is the anchor position, but the fund likely holds other cellular agriculture positions that provide diversification.

The synbio sector's brutal Darwinian selection process. Bolt Threads dead, Amyris bankrupt, Zymergen liquidated. Creates both the risk and the opportunity. The survivors are stronger, and Agronomics provides a way to bet on the survivor portfolio without picking individual winners.

Listed on the London Stock Exchange, making it accessible to most global retail investors. However, as a small-cap UK-listed fund, liquidity may be limited.

name: Unconventional AI
one_liner: Raised $475M seed at $4.5B valuation targeting 1,000x energy efficiency through brain-inspired analog computing.
thesis:
Unconventional AI raised a $475M seed round at $4.5B valuation (December 2025). One of the largest seed rounds in history. Founded by Naveen Rao, who sold MosaicML to Databricks for $1.3B and sold Nervana to Intel for $400M+. Backed by a16z, Lightspeed, Sequoia, Lux Capital, and Jeff Bezos.

The company is targeting 1,000x energy efficiency through brain-inspired analog computing, with first chips expected to tape out in 2026. The founder's track record, with two successful exits in the AI chip space. Is the primary reason investors deployed $475M at seed stage.

The investor roster is effectively a hall of fame. A16z, Lightspeed, Sequoia, Lux Capital, and Jeff Bezos personally. This level of backing at seed stage is extraordinarily rare and signals deep conviction in both the founder and the market opportunity.

Private company with no path to retail access in the near term. The $4.5B seed valuation means the next round will need to show significant technical progress to justify a markup. Watch for the tape-out announcement in 2026 as a key milestone.

name: ESS Inc
one_liner: Iron flow battery maker in survival mode at $31M market cap with 22-hour storage, zero degradation over 25 years, and Honeywell backing.
thesis:
ESS Inc builds iron flow batteries. A long-duration energy storage technology that offers 22-hour storage capability with zero degradation over 25 years, differentiating it sharply from lithium-ion alternatives that degrade significantly over their lifespan. The technology uses earth-abundant iron, salt, and water as its active materials, making it both low-cost and environmentally benign at end-of-life.

However, the company is in survival mode. The stock trades at $1.15 with a market cap of just $31M, down from a 52-week high of $13.87. FY2025 revenue was a mere $1.6M, down 75% year-over-year. Cash stands at $22M with working capital of approximately $1M, triggering going concern signals. The company is pivoting to its "Energy Base" product targeting 2027 commercialization. On the positive side, ESS has an SRP pilot with Google, 1.2 GWh in proposals worth approximately $400M, and strategic backing from Honeywell.

This is a high-risk turnaround play. The iron flow battery technology is genuinely differentiated, but the company must survive long enough to reach commercialization. The extreme valuation compression from $13.87 to $1.15 creates potential asymmetric upside if the Energy Base pivot succeeds, but the going concern risk is real and near-term.

name: Kamino Finance
one_liner: S38 40-AGENT VALIDATED: #1 Solana lending ($3.2B TVL, $14.3M rev) but ZERO value accrual to KMNO. Revenue declining -67%. Excellent protocol, worthless token.
thesis:
Sprint 38 40-agent validation confirms and strengthens Sprint 21 verdict: excellent protocol, worthless token.

Kamino Finance is objectively the #1 lending protocol on Solana. $3.2B TVL across lending ($1.5-2.1B) and liquidity vaults (~$1B). Lend V2 is genuinely impressive, modular architecture, isolated markets, RWA support ($1.23B). Institutional adoption is real: Anchorage Digital tri-party custody ($201M pilot), Bitwise $259M fund integration, Ethena $1B+ deposits. 18+ security audits with zero exploits and a $1.5M bug bounty. The protocol executes flawlessly.

The token captures none of this.

ZERO fee sharing, no governance proposal has ever been submitted. ZERO buyback or burn mechanism. Staking is emissions-only: "stake KMNO to earn more KMNO." The $14.33M in annualized protocol revenue flows entirely to LPs and the team treasury. Revenue is actually declining sharply: $35.7M in Q1 2025 → $11.9M in Q2 2026, a -67% collapse. The token has fallen -94% from its $0.2472 ATH (Dec 15, 2024) despite the protocol being dominant.

Making it worse: 229M tokens/month are opening through April 2027 (only 47% circulating), creating persistent sell pressure with zero offsetting demand. The P/S ratio of 4.97x isn't even cheap relative to Solana peers (Jupiter 3.91x, Raydium 3.41x), and those competitors actually have token value accrual mechanisms.

The team's track record includes Hubble Protocol, where HBB token fell -96% and USDH stablecoin was wound down. Anti-competitive behavior (blocking Jupiter Lend refinancing) suggests defensive positioning rather than confident market leadership.

Status: WAIT. Composite 42. Would reconsider ONLY if: (1) fee-sharing governance vote passes AND is implemented, (2) buyback/burn mechanism activated, or (3) token unlocks end and circulating supply stabilizes. Until then, the protocol is excellent and the token is worthless. This is NOT on the buying list.

name: Scallop Lend
one_liner: Lending protocol on Sui with $19.7M TVL. Early-stage play in the Sui DeFi system with institutional-grade risk management.
thesis:
Scallop Lend is the dominant lending protocol on the Sui blockchain, managing $577M in TVL across multiple money markets. As Sui's system has grown to become a top-10 chain by TVL, Scallop has captured the majority of lending demand.

The protocol differentiates itself through institutional-grade risk management, including tiered collateral factors, dynamic interest rate models, and real-time oracle aggregation across multiple price feeds. This conservative approach has attracted larger depositors who prioritize safety over yield maximization.

Scallop's position in the Sui system mirrors Aave's dominance on Ethereum. As new protocols launch on Sui and more assets become available for lending, Scallop benefits from network effects and liquidity depth. The SCA token governs the protocol and captures a portion of lending fees.

The investment case depends on Sui's continued system growth. With SUI token performance strong and developer activity increasing, the chain is attracting DeFi protocols, NFT platforms, and gaming applications that all need lending infrastructure. However, Sui remains a newer chain with less battle-tested security than Ethereum, and competition from NAVI Protocol and other Sui-native lenders is intensifying.
catalysts: Sui system TVL growth attracting new lending demand
Cross-margin features enabling capital efficiency
SCA token staking rewards and fee capture
risks: Sui blockchain security less battle-tested than Ethereum
Competition from NAVI Protocol on Sui
SCA token liquidity limited to Sui-native DEXs

name: Rain AI
one_liner: In distress despite Sam Altman's backing. $150M Series B collapsed in Q1 2025, reportedly exploring a sale to OpenAI, NVIDIA, or Microsoft.
thesis:
Rain AI is in distress. Despite Sam Altman's backing, their $150M Series B collapsed in Q1 2025. The company is reportedly exploring a sale. OpenAI, NVIDIA, and Microsoft are reportedly interested in acqui-hiring.

Rain AI was developing neuromorphic processing chips inspired by the brain's architecture. The collapse of the $150M Series B is a severe signal. When a company with Sam Altman's backing cannot close a raise, the technology or business fundamentals have likely been found wanting by sophisticated investors.

The acqui-hire interest from OpenAI, NVIDIA, and Microsoft suggests the talent and IP have value even if the standalone company does not. An acquisition could provide some return to existing investors, but likely at a steep discount to previous valuations.

This is effectively a distressed asset. Included as a graveyard entry given the collapsed fundraise and sale exploration. The neuromorphic chip space is being won by companies with better execution (Unconventional AI, Lightmatter) while Rain AI struggled to deliver.

name: CryoDAO
one_liner: Cryopreservation-focused BioDAO within the Bio Protocol system, now trading at ~$293K market cap in the micro-cap dead zone.
thesis:
CryoDAO is a BioDAO within the Bio Protocol system focused on cryopreservation research. The science of preserving biological tissue at ultra-low temperatures. Originally reported to have a $20M+ treasury, the token has crashed 98% from ATH, leaving it at ~$0.11 with a market cap of approximately $293K.

Cryopreservation is a legitimate field of scientific research with applications in organ transplantation, fertility preservation, and theoretical whole-body preservation. The DAO model allows community funding of research that traditional institutions may not prioritize due to the field's association with fringe cryonics movements. This is exactly the kind of 'dismissed as a toy' research area that DeSci is designed to fund.

However, the current state is dire. A $293K market cap places CryoDAO firmly in the micro-cap dead zone where liquidity is effectively zero, price discovery is absent, and any single trade can move the price dramatically. The gap between the reported $20M+ treasury and the current $293K market cap suggests either the treasury was denominated in tokens that have collapsed, or the market simply does not value the DAO's assets.

This is included for completeness as a Bio Protocol system component, but the investment case is extremely weak at current levels. Recovery would require either a significant cryopreservation research breakthrough funded by the DAO, or a broader DeSci sector revival lifting all boats.

name: Bolero
one_liner: Broadest IP approach to music tokenization on Polygon, covering master recordings rather than just streaming royalties.
thesis:
Bolero differentiates itself in the music tokenization space by taking the broadest possible approach to intellectual property. While competitors like Royal.io focus on streaming royalties and Opulous on royalty-backed DeFi loans, Bolero tokenizes master recordings themselves on Polygon. This gives token holders exposure to the full economic value of a recording. Including sync licensing, physical sales, and streaming. Rather than a single revenue stream.

The master recording approach is more ambitious but also more complex. Master recordings are the most valuable music IP assets, historically controlled by major labels and rarely made available to retail investors. By tokenizing masters rather than derivative rights, Bolero attempts to democratize access to the most coveted tier of music industry assets. The Polygon blockchain provides low transaction costs suitable for fractional ownership of these assets.

However, the project remains pre-token with limited public data on traction, team size, or specific partnerships. The music tokenization sector as a whole has failed to achieve mainstream adoption despite several years of experimentation by multiple platforms. The total tokenized music royalty market remains somewhere between $10-50M. A rounding error in the $30B+ annual music industry.

The investment thesis depends entirely on the sector achieving escape velocity. If music tokenization becomes a standard way for artists to raise capital and fans to invest in music, Bolero's master recording approach positions it at the highest-value segment. But this is a long-term bet with significant execution uncertainty and no near-term catalysts visible.

Master recording tokenization faces complex legal requirements across jurisdictions

name: Malta Inc
one_liner: Google X-origin pumped heat energy storage with Siemens partnership, but only 17 employees signal limited momentum.
thesis:
Malta Inc originated from Google X (Alphabet's moonshot lab) and is developing pumped heat energy storage (PHES) technology. The company has raised approximately $98-118M in funding and secured a partnership with Siemens. The 1000x Opportunity Map references a $50M Series B round. The technology converts electricity into thermal energy stored in molten salt and cold liquid, then reconverts it back to electricity when needed, offering potential for grid-scale storage.

The company has a full-scale heat exchanger testing program underway in Germany and a 14 MW demonstration plant in Spain. However, the most concerning data point is the team size of only 17 employees for a company that has raised nearly $100M+, this tiny team signals limited momentum and raises questions about capital efficiency and the pace of technical development.

Malta is pre-commercial with no clear path to market despite its Google X pedigree and Siemens partnership. The energy storage sector is intensely competitive, with Form Energy, Antora, and others moving faster toward commercialization with larger teams and more strong pipelines. The lack of named backers beyond the Google X origin and Siemens is a yellow flag. This is a watch-list entry rather than a near-term investment opportunity.

name: Story Protocol
one_liner: a16z-backed L1 for IP tokenization at $500M cap, generating less than $100 daily on-chain revenue.
thesis:
Story Protocol launched as an ambitious a16z-backed Layer 1 blockchain purpose-built for intellectual property management and tokenization. The $IP token trades at approximately $0.495 with a market cap of ~$500M, representing a catastrophic 96.7% decline from its all-time high of $14.89. Despite the prestigious backing, the protocol has failed to generate meaningful on-chain activity, with DeFiLlama reporting less than $100 in daily on-chain revenue. An extraordinarily low figure for a $500M market cap asset.

The fundamental problem facing Story Protocol is competitive displacement by C2PA (Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity), a non-blockchain standard that has achieved 6,000+ members and hardware-level adoption from Leica, Sony, Samsung, and Google Pixel cameras. C2PA is becoming the dominant standard for content provenance without requiring blockchain infrastructure, directly undermining Story Protocol's core value proposition. When the non-crypto solution is winning adoption at this pace, the on-chain alternative faces an uphill battle.

Internal challenges compound the external competitive threat. A co-founder departed in August 2025, and the company executed a 10% staff reduction in March 2026. These signals typically indicate either strategic pivoting or distress, neither of which supports the bull case for a protocol that needs aggressive growth to justify its valuation. The token unlock was delayed to August 2026, which could create additional selling pressure when it occurs.

The only remaining bull case is that on-chain IP tokenization provides capabilities (programmable royalties, permissionless licensing, global settlement) that C2PA cannot. If the creative economy evolves to require these features, Story Protocol's early infrastructure could become valuable. But the current metrics suggest the market has voted against this thesis, at least for now.

name: SSV Network
one_liner: DVT middleware that doesn't capture value from the ETH it secures, fees collapsed 99.7%
thesis:
SSV Network is the middleware layer that decentralizes Ethereum validator operations using Distributed Validator Technology (DVT). It splits a single validator key across 4+ operators, eliminating single points of failure in ETH staking.

The 406x TVL/MCap ratio is the standout metric: $617M+ in ETH secured by the protocol versus a $1.52M market cap. This is not a nascent project, SSV is production infrastructure that Lido, Rocket Pool, and institutional stakers depend on. DVT became an Ethereum roadmap requirement post-Merge, making SSV a critical layer for the network's long-term decentralization.

Ethereum's upcoming Pectra upgrade (EIP-7002) enables validator consolidation, which increases DVT demand as larger operators seek fault-tolerance without centralization. SSV DAO treasury holds $30M+ in reserves with no VC unlock pressure, entirely community-owned.

Sprint 19 verification: DOWNGRADED TO AVOID. The TVL/MCap ratio was the signal, but Sprint 19 deep research revealed a category error. SSV protocol fees have collapsed 99.7% as operators moved to the new permissionless v2 model with zero-fee structures. The $617M TVL is real, but the SSV token captures essentially none of it. This is infrastructure that works but cannot monetize. High TVL/MCap ratios mean nothing when the token's fee accrual mechanism is broken at the protocol level. AVOID until fee architecture is rebuilt.

name: Nectome
one_liner: Brain preservation startup largely dormant with 4 employees and ~$120K in funding. Brain uploading remains science fiction.
thesis:
Nectome is a brain preservation startup that aimed to develop technology for preserving brain connectomes for eventual digital reconstruction ('brain uploading'). The company is largely dormant with only 4 employees and approximately $120K in remaining funding.

Brain uploading remains firmly in the realm of science fiction. The fundamental challenges of mapping 86 billion neurons with 100+ trillion synaptic connections, understanding how memories are encoded, and recreating consciousness digitally. Are each individually decades away from solution, let alone in combination.

Nectome's trajectory from Y Combinator-backed startup to near-dormancy illustrates the gap between compelling science fiction narratives and investable technology. There are no credible investable plays in brain uploading.

This is a graveyard entry. Do not invest.
